channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: Is Discord Down Now? Discord Server Status and Network Guide
item.content:encoded:
Discord is one of the world’s most popular messenger applications. It’s a favourite amongst gamers, many of whom are members of copious numbers of Discord servers, and it’s the application of choice for those looking to play games online with their friends and stay connected in general. However, no system is perfect, and blips can often lead users to spam-search one very important question: ‘Is Discord down now?’

Fortunately, we have a one-stop, one-shot guide that’ll help you figure out the Discord server status at any given point. If you’re having network issues with Discord, look no further than this page.

Is Discord Offline?

That’s a great question. Is Discord down now, or are you alone in experiencing some bizarre network issues that are stopping you from talking to your cherished friends?

On March 9, Discord experienced issues, per the support page:

If you want to know the Discord server status as quickly as possible, the best way to do it is by using the resource set up and provided by the company itself. It’s a simple webpage that’s kept separate from the main application, so if the application is down, it’s almost guaranteed that this Discord server status page is unaffected.

Here’s the link you’ll need:

  • Discord Server Status Page

You can also try Down Detector for a good example of the issues people are having:

  • Discord Down Detector

Outside of simply checking the Discord server status, you can also use that site to check notifications, the search function, voice chat features, and more. There are a lot of metrics on the page, plus a historical tracker of any issues reported or tracked by Discord. It’s one of the best server status pages offered by any technology or communications company.

How to Solve Discord Network Error – ‘Messages Failed to Load’

If you’re getting a network error on Discord, you’ll likely spend the next few minutes searching ‘Is Discord down’ on every search engine you can reach – but that’s not the only solution.

While checking social media channels is a good idea, it’s not the be-all and end-all. Firstly, check if Discord is working for you on another device – ideally, one that’s different from your failing device. If you’re trying to connect to Discord on a PC (and failing), see if it works on a mobile device. Also, check to see if your friends and family are having similar issues.

It’s a good idea to reboot your device or the application, reset your router, perform a cache clearance or a power cycle, or just chill out and come back in a few minutes. If all else fails and Discord is offline for you for a very long time, consider reaching out to the team via the Discord support function.

Ironically, you can discuss this topic and more on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, find out if the PlayStation Network is down

Written by

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant's adoration for gaming started on the Amstrad CPC in 1996. Now, he's a multi-platform expert gamer with a deep love for the industry, a passion for esports, and more…

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

MOBILE APP

https://insider-gaming.com/is-discord-down-now-checker/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: jL to Stand-In for Liquid at Portuguese LAN
item.content:encoded:
Benched NAVI rifler, Justinas ‘⁠jL⁠’ Lekavicius, will stand-in for Liquid in place of Guy ‘NertZ⁠’ Iluz at Roman Imperian Cup VI in a crucial event for Liquid’s chances of making the IEM Cologne 2026 Major. NertZ misses the event due to issues regarding travel, but is expected to return for BLAST Open Rotterdam the following week.

HLTV broke the news following speculation from leakers who noted that jL was practising with Liquid instead of NertZ. However, the news was soon confirmed to not be a permanent roster move.

The report was confirmed by jL on X, who started that he was “Happy to be back even though it’s not for long.”

Liquid bank on former Major MVP after a seven-month absence

The Lithuanian rifler has been out of professional play since voluntarily benching himself in July 2025 in what was described by the Natus Vincere org as a ‘joint decision’ between the player and the org.

The Lithuanian would later release a video outlining his reasons for stepping away, in which the PGL Copenhagen Major MVP cited physical and mental health as reasons, as well as focus on his marriage and a desire to engage more with content creation.

The rifler has only played one HLTV match since his July benching, standing-in for fish123 in a 2-1 loss to G2’s academy team, G2 Ares, in August of last year.

Since the decision to step away, jL has engaged in Twitch streaming, most notably in collaboration with Counter-Strike skins creator ohnePixel, as well as working as an analyst at IEM Kraków.

He now reunites with Liquid coach, Viktor ‘⁠flashie⁠’ Tamás Bea, who was NAVI’s analyst until July 2025. The Hungarian has struggled to embed even the most fundamental habits in Liquid’s players, but can at least draw upon jL as a player he is familiar with.

At the very least, jL may bring some new energy to a Liquid roster that has looked defeated and unmotivated in recent times.

Liquid in a desperate race for Major qualification

Little is known about the Lithuanian’s individal form going into what may be a crucial event for Liquid’s season. The team is currently sitting as the 26th highest team in Europe’s regional rankings, a full 10 places below where they need to be to qualify for the Major with the April 6th cutoff looming large.

After the organisation’s perplexing decision to skip PGL Cluj-Napoca, combined with truly abject form that saw them bomb out of ESL Pro League Season 23 in Stage 1, Liquid are in desperate need of fresh LAN wins to grow their VRS points.

They sit #35 in the global VRS rankings, far below expectations for a Liquid team whose roster is still full of notable stars, including Intel Grand Slam winners in Jonathan ‘EliGE’ Jablonowski and Keith ‘NAF’ Markovic.

As such, Liquid will play local LANs in the form of Roman Imperian Cup VI from March 12-15 and DraculaN Season 6 from March 29-April 2 to try to notch up those much-needed LAN wins.

Liquid will attend BLAST Open Rotterdam from March 18-29 between the two LAN events, starting with a tough match-up against Danil ‘donk’ Kryshkovets’ Team Spirit.

Do you think jL will help Liquid’s terrible vibes? You can discuss that and more on our Discord server

Get more on the latest Counter-Strike news, such as PGL’s 2027 and 2028 event roadmap

Darragh is an Esports Journalist for Insider Gaming specialising in Counter-Strike. He loves to explore how esports teams work, or why they very often do not.

More from Darragh Harbinson
